name: Public cloneable() Method Without Final ('Object Hijack')
description: A class has a public cloneable() method that is not declared final, allowing objects to be created without invoking the constructor. This enables object hijacking where an attacker can bypass initialization procedures and security checks normally enforced during construction.
detection_pattern: STRUCTURAL(class) implementing Cloneable with a public clone method that is not final. Look for EXTERNAL(attacker)→TRANSFORM(clone) that bypasses AUTH(constructor) to create a new STORAGE(object) instance without proper initialization.
